items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 22 at 8:25PM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow. Additional snow accumulations up to one inch. Winds
gusting as high as 40 mph.

* WHERE...Lower Yukon River and Yukon Delta Coast.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted. Areas of blowing snow could
significantly reduce visibility.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Another pulse of wind will move into the area
tonight with gusts up to 40 mph. Temperatures during the second
round of wind will be in the low to mid 30s, so blowing snow will
be limited.
items.areas: Yukon Delta Coast; Lower Yukon River
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 22 at 8:25PM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow. Additional snow accumulations up to
two inches.

* WHERE...Eastern Norton Sound and Nulato Hills, Interior Seward
Peninsula, and Southern Seward Peninsula Coast.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Visibility may drop to 1/2 mile at times
tonight and early tomorrow morning. With temperatures expected to
rise into the 30s by tomorrow morning and near 40 for some in the
afternoon, blowing snow will quickly
items.areas: Southern Seward Peninsula Coast; Interior Seward Peninsula; Eastern Norton Sound and Nulato Hills
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.

items.headline: Blizzard Warning issued April 22 at 8:25PM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Blizzard conditions. Additional snow accumulations up to
two inches. Winds gusting as high as 35 mph.

* WHERE...Baldwin Peninsula, Lower Kobuk Valley, and Northern Seward
Peninsula.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest winds will continue through
tonight. Wind gusts will remain around 35 mph through Thursday
night. Temperatures will rise into the 30s Thursday afternoon and
blowing snow will become less of a concern.
items.areas: Lower Kobuk Valley; Baldwin Peninsula; Northern Seward Peninsula
items.instruction:
Travel should be restricted to emergencies only. If you must travel,
have a winter survival kit with you. If you get stranded, stay with
your vehicle.

items.headline: Blizzard Warning issued April 22 at 8:25PM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Blizzard conditions. Additional snow accumulations up to
one inch. Winds gusting as high as 45 mph.

* WHERE...Bering Strait Coast.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be difficult. Areas of blowing snow could
significantly reduce visibility.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest winds will continue through the
early overnight and the warning may be downgraded to an advisory.
Temperatures will rise into the 30s Thursday afternoon and blowing
snow will become less o
items.instruction:
Travel should be restricted to emergencies only. If you must travel,
have a winter survival kit with you. If you get stranded, stay with
your vehicle. Visit 511.alaska.gov or call 5 1 1 for the latest road
conditions.

items.headline: Blizzard Warning issued April 22 at 8:25PM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Blizzard conditions. Additional snow accumulations between
3 and 5 inches. Winds gusting as high as 45 mph.

* WHERE...St Lawrence Island.

* WHEN...Until 4 AM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult. Areas of blowing snow
could significantly reduce visibility. Strong winds could result
in power outages.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Wind gusts will remain elevated, up to 45 mph
through Thursday afternoon. Temperatures Thursday afternoon will
warm into the low to mid 30s and
items.instruction:
Travel should be restricted to emergencies only. If you must travel,
have a winter survival kit with you. If you get stranded, stay with
your vehicle.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 22 at 5:17PM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Blowing snow expected. Additional snow accumulations up to
one inch. Winds gusting as high as 40 mph.

* WHERE...Western Arctic Coast.

* WHEN...Until 4 AM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted. Areas of blowing snow could
significantly reduce visibility to 1/2 mile or less at times.
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 22 at 5:17PM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK

Winds gusting as high as 35 mph.

items.headline: Flood Watch issued April 22 at 12:36PM AKDT until April 25 at 11:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by rain and snowmelt is possible.

* WHERE...The following areas, Central Susitna Valley, Southern
Susitna Valley and Western Susitna Valley.  This includes the
communities of Skwentna, Willow, Talkeetna, Trapper Creek, And
Petersville.

* WHEN...From 9 PM AKDT this evening through Saturday morning.

* IMPACTS...Flooding of creeks, streams, low-lying and flood-prone
locations will be possible as a prolonged period of rainfall is
expected Wednesday night into Saturday mor
items.areas: Western Susitna Valley; Southern Susitna Valley; Central Susitna Valley
items.instruction:
Persons living along streams should stay alert and be prepared to
take action if a warning or advisory is issued, or flooding is
observed.

You should monitor later forecasts and be alert for possible Flood
Warnings. Those living in areas prone to flooding should be prepared
to take action should fl

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 22 at 12:24PM AKDT until April 23 at 1:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow expected. Total snow accumulations up
to two inches. Winds gusting as high as 40 mph.

* WHERE...Dalton Highway Summits.

* WHEN...From 4 PM this afternoon to 1 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted. Areas of blowing snow could
significantly reduce visibility.
items.instruction:
Slow down and use caution while traveling. Visit 511.alaska.gov or
call 5 1 1 for the latest road conditions.

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 22 at 11:48AM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 35 to 45 mph with gusts up to 65 mph expected.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...From 10 PM this evening to 4 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.areas: Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.
